rag_rules: Place assume/guarantee declarations in component type scopes when describing interface behavior.
Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks to contain AGREE contract elements.
generic_rules: Use visible identifiers and named constants in AGREE expressions to ensure scope validity.
Equation and constant declarations should reference valid AADL types and features.
Assignments and bindings must be placed in implementation scopes after subcomponents.
Avoid encoding AADL properties as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts use `assume`, `guarantee`, `eq`, `const`, and `assign` syntax within `annex agree` blocks.
Component type contracts define interface behavior; implementation contracts define internal bindings.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level `assign` clauses in component type scopes.
Do not reference AADL property names like Deadline or Period directly in AGREE expressions.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and their automatic scope in AGREE
